course_description:
This course provides students with the fundamental theories and models to develop a sound understanding of consumers, their needs, desires and behaviours. Study in this course will encourage students to examine individual and group decision-making, buying experiences and the interrelationships between the individual consumer, consumption practices and lifestyle activities.

A fundamental marketing principle is the centrality of the customer to organizational planning;ﾠthus an understanding of the perceptions, attitudes and behaviours of customers is critical to successful marketing strategy and advertising campaigns. MKTG2501 is designed to provide students with knowledge of the behavioural, cognitive, affective, and cultural approaches to consumer behaviour; the facets of consumer motivation, personality and attitudes; the influences of society and culture; an awareness of the critical implications of these processes to marketing actions; and an ability to translate and apply consumer theory to analyse consumer responses and market development and change.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

Sustainable Development Goals - UQ Business School is a proud supporter and Advanced Signatory of the United Nations Principles for Responsible Management Education (UN PRME) . As part of the largest global collaboration between business schools and the UN, the school emphasises its role in empowering students to drive societal transformation through the Sustainable Development Goals . The SDGs highlight that a thriving economy relies on a healthy environment, aiming to balance economic growth, social well-being, and environmental protection for a sustainable future.

course_aims: The aim of this course is to develop the student's understanding of consumer behaviour, building on the basic principles that were introduced in MKTG1501. Students should gain an understanding of the major theoretical issues and concepts relating to the sub−discipline of Consumer Behaviour, as well as an understanding of the practical applications of these theories and concepts to marketing practice.

learning_outcomes.description: Interpret and illustrate cognitive, behavioural, and cultural theories that inform an understanding of consumer behaviour.
Explain and distinguish the internal and external factors that influence consumer behaviour (e.g., decision-making, attitudes, personality, lifestyle, social class, consumer culture).
Analyse real-world problems for relevant ethical and evidence-based marketing solutions.
As a cooperative group, plan, conduct and present primary and secondary research appropriate for a specific consumer behaviour enquiry.
Select and use appropriate levels and means of communication.

assessment_summary.title: Case Study Research
assessment_summary.due_date: Planning document submission in Week 6 2/04/2026; Team presentations completed during tutorials Week 12 - Week 13

assessment_details.title: In-Semester Examination
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The in-semester exam will assess content from lectures and required readings (Week 1 to Week 4), as well as tutorial material (Week 2 to Week 5). The exam will be paper-based, held on a Saturday outside scheduled teaching times, and invigilated on campus. The exam will include FOUR short-answer questions and ONE essay-style question. The essay question will require students to apply their understanding of consumer behaviour to analyse or respond to a marketing problem. Students may bring ONE handwritten single-page A4 sheet of notes into the exam. Additional guidance on exam preparation will be provided in lectures and through applied activities in weekly tutorials. AI Statement This assessment must be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools is not permitted. Any attempted use of these tools may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Case Study Research
assessment_details.due_date: Planning document submission in Week 6 2/04/2026 Team presentations completed during tutorials Week 12 - Week 13
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor, Longitudinal. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: For this assessment, students will identify a marketing problem and develop a problem-based consumer behaviour case study. The case study should explain how consumers’ perceptions, attitudes, motivations, cultures or behaviours influence purchasing, social actions and decision-making. Students must collect primary research by conducting THREE consumer interviews, supported by secondary research drawn from academic journals, industry reports and marketing literature. The assessment has two components: PART A: Planning Document (10%) – Submission due Week 6 The planning document outlines how information and data will be collected for the written case study. PART B: Case Study Presentation (35%) – Submission due Week 12 Teams will present their case study findings during tutorials in Weeks 12 and 13. All team members must present for the presentations. Presentations are 20 minutes (15 minutes presenting and 5 minutes for Q&A). Required submissions include: Presentation slides (20%) Research portfolio (5%) , which acts as a supporting document demonstrating the team’s research process and evidence base There will be a 10% individual mark , influenced by presentation performance and demonstrated contribution to teamwork. This includes evidence from the team contract (Week 4) and peer evaluations, which will inform adjustments where necessary. Teamwork Teams will be formed in Week 2 and finalised in Week 3, with the team list and topic submitted to the tutor. Teams must be formed within the same tutorial group and may include up to FIVE students. Students who enrol late or do not engage early may be required to complete the assessment individually or in a smaller team. Teams are expected to monitor contributions throughout the semester and raise issues with the tutor promptly. Where individuals do not contribute, marks may be deducted to reflect their participation, including the possibility of receiving zero. Students should keep records of their work to verify contributions. AI Statement Students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools for this assessment. Any use of AI or MT must be clearly referenced each time. Failure to reference these tools may be considered student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Follow assessment links from blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions or deferrals are not available for an in-class presentation. An extension may be available for the submitted material only.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
10% Late Penalty applies to submitted material only. Late submissions are not accepted for in-class presentations. Failure to present at the scheduled time will result in a mark of zero for the presentation portion of this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Final Examination
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The final exam will assess content covered in lectures and required readings from Week 6 to Week 12. The exam will be paper-based and invigilated on campus. The exam will consist of 25 multiple-choice questions (MCQs) that require students to apply their knowledge of consumer behaviour to interpret, analyse or respond to marketing problems. Preparation for the final exam will be discussed in lectures. AI Statement This assessment must be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools is not permitted. Attempted use of these tools may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
